source: v2.8 Part G — Checker Calibration Notes: Known False Positive Patterns
scope: Universal calibration notes — apply across all commodities and routes

entries.title: Z-STRIKES
entries.description: Z-strikes may appear as undeleted text in low-resolution scans. Large diagonal Z-strikes spanning multiple sub-clauses are a valid Method 1 deletion technique. Do not raise a hard error on deletion completeness from scan appearance alone when Z-strikes are present. Request enlarged/higher-resolution image before raising a deletion hard error based on scan appearance.

entries.title: BLANK FIELDS
entries.description: Blank/white fields are Method 3 redactions, not unstruck blanks. On digitally prepared EHCs, unused fields and unused commodity table rows are deleted by redaction (Method 3). Do not flag blank/white unused rows or fields as unstruck. Only flag if: (a) a strikethrough line is visible but incomplete (edge miss), or (b) a row contains partial data with no deletion marking.

entries.title: WORKSHEETS
entries.description: Worksheets are working documents — judge only on the signed EHC. Pick sheets, load sheets and dispatch documents may contain multiple edits, corrections, different OV references, rolled date changes, and incomplete fields. They are not the signed certificate. Content on a worksheet must never trigger a hard error on the certificate.

entries.title: OV NUMBERING SYSTEMS
entries.description: SP number and RCVS number are different systems for the same OV. SP number — APHA-issued OV reference, used on circular stamps. RCVS number — Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons registration, typically shown on the qualification stamp on the signing page. Do not flag as a mismatch or as two different OVs.

entries.title: SCAN QUALITY
entries.description: Do not raise hard errors based on scan appearance alone for ink colour or stamp clarity. Ink colour (black vs dark blue) cannot be reliably determined from a scanned PDF. Only flag stamp or signature ink as black if it is unambiguously black on the physical document — not based on scan appearance.

entries.title: VEHICLE & TRAILER IDENTIFICATION
entries.description: Trailer plate in I.19 container field is acceptable practice on curtainsider road vehicle loads. Foreign plates (Lithuanian, Bulgarian, Polish, Romanian, Belgian, Dutch etc.) in I.15 and I.19 are expected for international haulage. Vehicle registration cross-check: compare alphanumeric characters only — spaces, hyphens, dots and country prefixes in handwritten dispatch notes are optional formatting variations and never cause a mismatch. Handwriting variations resolved by photo — photo is ground truth.

entries.title: ROUTE & BCP COMBINATIONS
entries.description: Destination country and BCP entry point may differ — this is not an error. A load may enter the EU at one BCP country but be destined for a different EU member state. Language check is driven by BCP country (I.16), not destination country (I.9).

entries.title: PDF EDITABLE FIELD FAINT PRINTING
entries.description: All EU EHC templates print editable field text in a lighter/greyer font than the pre-printed text. This is a universal template characteristic — not an error or omission. Before flagging any field as blank or illegible from a scan, check the opposite language section or request a photograph. Never raise a notice on field content based on scan faintness alone.

entries.title: SIGNING PAGE POSITIONAL FLEXIBILITY
entries.description: All signing area elements (date, name/qualifications, stamp, signature) need only be present somewhere within the signing area on the page. Exact positional alignment to the printed field labels is not required and must not be flagged. A date handwritten anywhere within the signing area is always a pass — do not raise any flag, medium warning or otherwise, where the correct date is legible within the signing area. This applies to all EHC types and all OVs.

entries.title: AMR ATTESTATION (II.1a)
entries.description: On 8468 and 8384 certificates, the AMR attestation is not applicable until 3 September 2026. Both full redaction (blank/white field, Method 3) AND pen strikethrough (Method 1 with adjacent stamp) are confirmed normal deletion methods. Do not flag either method.

entries.title: VESSEL / RO-RO LOADS (I.15 N/A)
entries.description: On vessel/ro-ro consignments, I.15 Means of transport will show Vessel ticked (often alongside Road vehicle) and Identification field as N/A. This is correct — the trailer registration appears in I.19 Container No field. Do not flag I.15 Identification as N/A on these loads.

entries.title: COMBINED SEAL/TRAILER PHOTO
entries.description: A single photo showing the seal number, seal integrity and trailer plate in one frame is accepted as full verification of all three elements. Separate photos are not required if the combined shot is clear and all three elements are legible. Photo remains ground truth.

entries.title: SEAL PHOTO NOTE / BATCH ANNOTATION MISMATCH
entries.description: Where a handwritten note accompanies seal photos, the batch reference on the note may reflect a prior aborted load if the same tanker and seals were carried over to a subsequent consignment. The note is not a controlled document. Verify the batch number against the EHC and delivery note only — if those agree and the physical seal numbers in the photo match I.19, the discrepancy on the note is a low notice at most and not a hold reason.

entries.title: DSV TANKER RANGE DSV533031-DSV533040: FOUR SEAL REQUIREMENT
entries.description: Tankers in the range DSV533031 to DSV533040 inclusive are a new build series fitted with three top vents. Standard tankers (single vent): 2 seals required (1 vent + 1 rear). DSV533031-533040 (triple vent): 4 seals required (3 vents + 1 rear). When an EHC is submitted with any tanker in this range, apply a MEDIUM WARNING. Verify that I.19 records exactly four seal numbers. If fewer than four seals are recorded, escalate to hard error. AMENDED v2.9: in-situ seal photos required — H&S exception withdrawn. AMENDED v3.6: range is INCLUSIVE — DSV533031, DSV533032, DSV533033, DSV533034, DSV533035, DSV533036, DSV533037, DSV533038, DSV533039 and DSV533040 all qualify. Out-of-range examples (e.g. DSV533018, DSV533025, DSV533045) are STANDARD tankers requiring 2 seals only — do not apply this rule. All four seal numbers in the photos must match I.19 and the DN exactly; any discrepancy = hard error (see E54).

entries.title: SAMPLE CONSIGNMENTS: PALLET STICKER SEALS ON INDIVIDUAL BAGS
entries.description: Where a small sample consignment (e.g. 4 bags) is dispatched with individual pallet sticker seals applied per bag, each seal number must appear in I.19. If the number of seals listed in I.19 does not match the number of bags/packages in I.24, flag as medium warning.

entries.title: OV/CO DELETION ON SIGNING PAGE: STAMP REQUIREMENT BY METHOD
entries.description:
The stamp-free exception for OV/CO deletion on the signing page applies ONLY to Method 3 redaction (text removal/whiteout). Where the OV/CO choice is deleted by hand pen strikethrough (Method 1) or Adobe digital strikethrough (Method 2), a stamp and initials ARE required adjacent to that deletion — identical to the general per-deletion rule at A7.1. Failure to stamp a Method 1 or Method 2 OV/CO deletion on the signing page = hard error.

Deletion method for OV/CO on signing page | Adjacent stamp required?
- Method 3 — redaction (text removal): No
- Method 1 — hand pen strikethrough: Yes — stamp + initials
- Method 2 — Adobe strikethrough: Yes — stamp + initials

entries.title: HIGH-RISK PAGE IDENTIFICATION FOR REFERENCE CONSISTENCY CHECK
entries.description: Rule A4 applies to all pages equally, but the highest-risk pages for certificate reference mismatch are: (1) the first page of each language section — where consignee, consignor, transport and BCP details are entered and pages are most often reprinted after a loading change; and (2) any page containing the commodity description table or product weight details — where a damage event, short-pick, or weight revision requires a reprint. When checking A4, pay particular attention to these pages in both the English and second language sections. Flag any mismatch on any page as a hard error and note the specific page number.

entries.title: I.26 GROSS WEIGHT: POPULATION REQUIREMENT
entries.description: Net weight must always be populated — hard error if absent (no change). Gross weight must also be populated on all non-tanker loads: for all bagged powder, canned product, cooked meat, composite products, petfood, and hatching egg consignments, absence of gross weight = medium warning. AMENDED v3.6: For bulk tanker EHCs, absence of gross weight is a SILENT PASS — no flag of any kind at any severity level, and not to be mentioned in the report at all. Do not raise any notice on bulk tanker gross weight absence.

entries.title: EN / SECOND LANGUAGE PARITY CHECK: UNIVERSAL CHECK SEQUENCE REQUIREMENT
entries.description: The parity check between the English and second language deletion patterns must be performed as a discrete step in the check sequence on all bilingual EHC types (8468, 8322, 8384, 8324, 8436, 8471). Compare the deletion pattern clause by clause across both language versions. A deletion present in the English section but absent from the second language section (or vice versa) = hard error on all bilingual types. Report any parity failure with the specific clause reference and the language section where the deletion is missing. This rule already existed within individual certificate Parts (C7, K6, L6 etc.) — E47 makes it explicit as a universal check sequence step.

entries.title: AMENDED/VERSION-SUFFIXED EHC FILENAMES
entries.description: Where an EHC has been amended after initial issue, the filename will include a prefix or suffix such as "amended_", "v2", "_corrected" or similar, to distinguish the revised document from the original. The checker must always verify they are working from the most recent version. If an unamended version is presented for a consignment known to have required amendment, flag as medium warning and request the current version. The amended file is the authoritative document for checking purposes.

entries.title: CURTAINSIDER SEAL COUNT: DISPATCH NOTE TEMPLATE WORDING
entries.description: Dispatch notes on AFI/Gregory's curtainsider loads carry standard template text such as "5 × curtainsider / 1 × container" in the seals field. This describes available seal positions, not the number of seals actually applied. Do not raise a flag based on this wording. The seal photo is ground truth for the number of seals used — if one seal appears in the photo and one seal number is recorded in I.19, this is correct and complete. No flag of any kind.

entries.title: SP STAMP DIGIT MISREAD ON NOTES PAGES
entries.description: On lower-resolution scans of notes/footnotes pages, SP stamp digits may render ambiguously — particularly "3" reading as "5" in SP 136830. Do not escalate as a hard error where the discrepancy is consistent with scan degradation and all other pages clearly show the correct SP number. Apply A7 NOTE: only escalate if the number is clearly and unambiguously different.

entries.title: DUPLICATE/EXTRA PAGES ON ROLLED LOADS
entries.description: When checking any EHC, verify that the actual PDF page count matches the declared "X of N" pagination on every page. A document containing more physical pages than the declared total — including duplicate signing pages inserted during a roll/reissue — is a hard error. The superseded page must be removed before dispatch. Particular vigilance required on rolled loads where only selected pages have been reprinted.

entries.title: AFI TANKER EHC: DELIVERY NOTE MANDATORY
entries.description: For all AFI bulk tanker EHCs (identified by I.1 consignor Arla Foods Ingredients Group P/S and manufacturing plant GB DE 030 or similar AFI establishment), the Delivery Note must be uploaded to the checker. If the DN is not uploaded, raise as a MEDIUM WARNING — the load must not depart until the DN has been cross-checked against I.19 seal numbers, vehicle identification, and batch/weight details. Source: v2.9 calibration following an AFI Variolac DN/seal mismatch incident on 24 April 2026 where the factory issued a DN with an incorrect seal number; the EHC was correct, the fault lay with the commercial document, and the error was not detectable without the DN being presented to the checker. Mandatory DN upload closes this gap.

entries.title: AFI TANKER AND DSV533031-533040: IN-SITU SEAL PHOTO CROSS-CHECK — HARD ERROR
entries.description: For all AFI bulk tanker EHCs and all DSV533031-533040 range tankers, in-situ seal photos must be uploaded to the checker. The seal number(s) visible in the photo(s) must be cross-checked against: (1) I.19 on the EHC; (2) the seal number(s) on the Delivery Note; and (3) any other photo evidence uploaded. ANY discrepancy between any of these sources — photo vs EHC, photo vs DN, EHC vs DN — is a HARD ERROR. HOLD the load. For DSV533031-533040 (four seals) all four seal numbers must appear in photos and match EHC and DN. AMENDED v3.6: AFI LPC Taw Valley REAR SEAL exception — for all AFI tanker types on the Taw Valley lane, top vent seal(s) must have in-situ photos but rear seal photo is NOT required (document pack is placed behind the rear seal at time of checking). Rear seal absence from photos = silent pass. Rear seal number must still appear in I.19 and DN. AMENDED v3.6: any legible presentation of all seal numbers across DN fields is accepted — DN formatting variation alone is not a flag (see also E55 zero-padding). Source: v2.9 + v3.6 calibration.

entries.title: AFI TANKER DN SEAL FIELD ZERO-PADDING
entries.description: The AFI Delivery Note 'Seal No sale' field omits leading zeros (e.g. records '546679' rather than '00546679'). This is a known DN formatting characteristic. When cross-checking EHC vs DN vs photo, compare significant digits only. Zero-padding difference alone = no flag of any kind. Genuine digit mismatch between significant digits = hard error per E54.

entries.title: BOXED TYPE SUFFIX ON OVERSEAS TRAILER IDENTIFICATION
entries.description: European curtainsider trailers display a registration number followed by a type/category digit in a separate box (e.g. TIPE 589108 [0]). When rendered as a single string on the EHC (e.g. TIPE 5891080), the trailing digit is the boxed type indicator, not a transcription error. Where the photo confirms this pattern, pass at all severity levels. No flag of any kind.

entries.title: AIR FREIGHT / COURIER SAMPLE CONSIGNMENTS: I.15 IDENTIFICATION
entries.description: On air-freighted sample consignments, I.15 Identification will show a courier waybill reference rather than a vehicle or trailer registration. This is accepted practice for this consignment type. Generate a low notice for awareness on first instance on a new lane; treat as standing pass thereafter once the lane is confirmed. Source: v3.6 calibration.

entries.title: FORTHGLADE/RENSKE GROUPAGE: MULTIPLE EHCs ON SINGLE TRAILER
entries.description: Forthglade/Renske petfood consignments to Netherlands via Calais are routinely split across two or three EHCs on a single trailer, divided per the F3 species category rules: RUM_BOV_OV (any line containing bovine/ovine/caprine Ruminantia), RUM_VEN (venison lines only, where present), and AVES_PESCA/MAM (all remaining lines with no ruminant content). All EHCs in the set share the same I.23 seal number and container reference — correct and expected. Weights, package counts and schedules are per-certificate only; never cross-add totals between certificates. Each EHC is checked independently against its own schedule. Source: v3.8 calibration.

entries.title: A9 SIGNING PAGE VISUAL CHECK — HUMAN VERIFICATION REQUIRED
entries.description: AI-assisted checking of stamp and signature compliance (A7/A9) from compressed or lower-resolution scanned PDFs carries an elevated risk of missing absent elements — particularly qualification stamps or handwritten name/qualification on second-language signing pages, where field content may be sparse and faint. Structured field-by-field rules, weight arithmetic, batch number format, seal cross-checks and EN/second language parity checks are reliable from scan. A9 signing page completeness — specifically the presence of name and qualification on both EN and second language signing pages — must always receive a final human visual check before dispatch clearance is confirmed. Do not rely solely on AI output for A9 compliance. Source: v3.6 calibration.
